g.text: * This cuts power for the TPD chip when USB-C is not connected.

g.desc: * This cuts power for the TPD chip when USB-C is not connected.

g.text: The TPD*S300A needs to be powered together with the MCU

g.desc: The TPD*S300A needs to be powered together with the MCU

g.text: (the USB-PD controller), hence why an external power is

g.desc: (the USB-PD controller), hence why an external power is

g.text: necessary, and why it's the same 3.3V from the MCU.

g.desc: necessary, and why it's the same 3.3V from the MCU.

g.text: * The board supports Source and Sink roles, including DRP. The

g.desc: * The board supports Source and Sink roles, including DRP. The

g.text: TPD will initially be powered, but considering its rather

g.desc: TPD will initially be powered, but considering its rather

g.text: high quiescent, it is a good idea to turn off the TPD

g.desc: high quiescent, it is a good idea to turn off the TPD

g.text: whenever the USB-PD role or MCU's mode of operation allows

g.desc: whenever the USB-PD role or MCU's mode of operation allows

g.text: [input-vbus] USB-C Connector: Protections, Filter and VUSB Discharge

g.desc: [input-vbus] USB-C Connector: Protections, Filter and VUSB Discharge

g.text: * Maybe it needs proper calculation for this Pi-like filter... Or perhaps it matters not.

g.desc: * Maybe it needs proper calculation for this Pi-like filter... Or perhaps it matters not.

g.text: * ESD is bidirectional because it's what I have in stock, but it can be replaced with a uni.

g.desc: * ESD is bidirectional because it's what I have in stock, but it can be replaced with a uni.
